name: Honey
description: Sweet golden honey gathered from sparkling flower beds and Combee habitats. A versatile crafting and cooking ingredient beloved by Bug-type Pokemon.
howToObtain: Interact with sparkling flower beds in any area, or collect from Combee habitat Litter boxes.
farmingTip: Build Combee habitats early and check their Litter regularly. Sparkling flower beds respawn daily across all four main areas.

name: Sturdy Stick
description: A solid wooden stick found while exploring or chopping wood. One of the most basic and frequently needed crafting materials in Pokopia.
howToObtain: General exploring drops, use Cut on wood objects, or collect from Cacturne drops.
farmingTip: Cut every piece of wood you see in Withered Wasteland. Cacturne drops these reliably if you have one as a companion.

name: Stone
description: A plain chunk of stone found everywhere. Essential building block for walls, paths, and countless crafting recipes.
howToObtain: General exploring drops, use Rock Smash on stone blocks, or collect from Rock-type Pokemon Litter boxes.
location: All main areas, especially Rocky Ridges
farmingTip: Rock-type Pokemon habitats produce Stone in their Litter constantly. Rock Smash every block you pass in Rocky Ridges for bulk gathering.

name: Leaf
description: A fresh green leaf gathered from vegetation. Used in basic crafting recipes and some early cooking dishes.
howToObtain: General exploring drops, use Cut on tall grass and vines, or collect from Venusaur drops.
farmingTip: Withered Wasteland and Bleak Beach have abundant tall grass. Venusaur is an excellent farming companion for Leaf drops.

name: Small Log
description: A short log cut from trees and worn lumber. The primary source material for Lumber when processed by a Pokemon with the Chop specialty.
howToObtain: Use Cut on trees and worn lumber throughout all areas. Also drops from Haxorus and appears in Haxorus Litter boxes.
farmingTip: Haxorus Litter boxes are the best passive source. For active farming, cycle through trees in Withered Wasteland since they regrow quickly.

name: Vine Rope
description: Strong natural rope made from cut vines. Used in furniture crafting, bridges, and various construction recipes.
howToObtain: Use Cut on vines growing on walls and structures. Bellsprout and Weepinbell also drop Vine Rope.
farmingTip: Vines regrow after a day. Bellsprout habitats with Litter boxes provide a steady passive supply without needing to explore.

name: Glowing Mushroom
description: A bioluminescent mushroom found in dark caves. Its soft glow makes it useful in lighting recipes and certain special crafts.
howToObtain: Found growing in caves throughout the Withered Wasteland and Rocky Ridges areas.
location: Caves in Withered Wasteland and Rocky Ridges
farmingTip: Cave mushrooms respawn regularly. The Wasteland Dream Island also has abundant Glowing Mushrooms in its underground sections.

name: Twine
description: Thin, sturdy string recovered from textile garbage or spun by Bug-type Pokemon. A staple crafting material for nets, furniture, and decorations.
howToObtain: Collect from textile garbage piles, Bug-type Pokemon Litter boxes, or by cutting cobwebs in caves.
farmingTip: Bug-type habitats are the most reliable source. Cutting cobwebs in caves yields Twine as a bonus while mining.

name: Sea Glass Fragment
description: Smooth, frosted glass pieces found on polluted coastal tiles. Once cleaned, they become beautiful crafting materials for decorations and coastal furniture.
howToObtain: Found on muk-covered tiles at Bleak Beach. Clean up pollution to reveal the fragments beneath.
farmingTip: Systematically clean muk-covered tiles at Bleak Beach. Each cleaned tile has a chance to drop Sea Glass. Ocean Dream Island is another reliable source.

name: Seashell
description: A pretty shell washed ashore on the beach. Used in coastal-themed decorations and some crafting recipes.
howToObtain: Pick up along the shoreline at Bleak Beach.
farmingTip: Walk the full Bleak Beach shoreline each day. Shells respawn daily and are plentiful once you clear the pollution.

name: Squishy Clay
description: Soft, malleable clay dug from clay blocks. When processed with the Burn specialty, it becomes sturdy Brick for construction.
howToObtain: Break clay blocks found in various areas, or collect from Ground-type Pokemon Litter boxes.
farmingTip: Ground-type Litter boxes are the easiest passive source. Dig up clay blocks whenever you spot them to stockpile for Brick production.

name: Fluff
description: Soft, fluffy wool gathered from electric sheep Pokemon. Used in textile crafting recipes for furniture cushions and soft decorations.
howToObtain: Collect from Mareep and Flaaffy habitat Litter boxes.
farmingTip: Build Mareep habitats as soon as possible. Their Litter boxes produce Fluff consistently, making it one of the easiest materials to farm passively.

name: Wastepaper
description: Discarded paper scraps found among the urban ruins. Can be recycled into clean Paper for crafting wallpapers and decorations.
howToObtain: Found while exploring the Sparkling Skylands area.
farmingTip: Sparkling Skylands is loaded with Wastepaper. Collect it in bulk and Recycle it into Paper for wallpaper and decoration recipes.

name: Seaweed
description: Aquatic plant matter gathered from water areas. Used in certain crafting recipes and some cooking dishes.
howToObtain: Collected from water areas, particularly at Bleak Beach and anywhere you can Surf.
location: Bleak Beach, water areas in all regions
farmingTip: Once you learn Surf from Lapras at Bleak Beach, water areas across all regions become accessible for Seaweed gathering.

name: Nonburnable Garbage
description: Trash and debris cluttering the polluted beach. Can be recycled into useful Iron Ore rather than simply discarded.
howToObtain: Clean up pollution at Bleak Beach.
farmingTip: Do not throw this away. Use the Recycle ability to convert Nonburnable Garbage into Iron Ore, giving you a valuable secondary ore source.

name: Copper Ore
description: Raw copper extracted from copper-veined rock blocks in the mountains. Smelted into Copper Ingots for tools and decorations.
howToObtain: Mine copper blocks in Rocky Ridges using Rock Smash.
location: Rocky Ridges, Rock Peak Dream Island
farmingTip: Rocky Ridges has abundant copper blocks on its lower levels. Rock Peak Dream Island also has copper deposits for supplemental farming.

name: Iron Ore
description: Dense iron ore mined from the deeper sections of Rocky Ridges. The backbone of mid-game crafting when smelted into Iron Ingots.
howToObtain: Mine iron blocks in Rocky Ridges, or Recycle Nonburnable Garbage from Bleak Beach.
farmingTip: Combine active mining in Rocky Ridges with Recycling Nonburnable Garbage from Bleak Beach for maximum Iron Ore throughput.

name: Gold Ore
description: Precious gold ore found in the deepest mining areas. Smelted into Gold Ingots for premium furniture and decorations.
howToObtain: Mine gold blocks in the deeper sections of Rocky Ridges.
farmingTip: Gold blocks are rarer than copper or iron. Focus on the deepest mine levels in Rocky Ridges. Volcanic Dream Island has additional gold deposits.

name: Pokemetal Fragment
description: A mysterious metallic fragment unique to Pokopia. Found in special Pokemetal blocks that only appear in the most extreme environments. Smelted into Pokemetal for top-tier crafting.
howToObtain: Mine Pokemetal blocks found in Withered Wasteland and Sparkling Skylands.
location: Withered Wasteland, Sparkling Skylands, Sky Dream Island
farmingTip: Pokemetal blocks are scattered across Withered Wasteland and Sparkling Skylands. Sky Dream Island is the best Dream Island source. Smelt these into Pokemetal for endgame recipes.

name: Rare Pokemetal Fragment
description: An exceptionally pure piece of Pokemetal that occasionally drops from Pokemetal blocks. Extremely valuable and needed for the rarest crafting recipes.
howToObtain: Low drop rate from mining Pokemetal blocks in Withered Wasteland and Sparkling Skylands.
farmingTip: This is a rare drop, not guaranteed. Mine every Pokemetal block you find and be patient. You will need several for legendary-tier recipes.

name: Lumber
description: Processed wooden planks created when a Pokemon with the Chop specialty works on Small Logs. The primary building material for wooden structures and furniture.
howToObtain: Use the Chop specialty (Scyther or Haxorus) on Small Logs.
farmingTip: Recruit Scyther or Haxorus as soon as possible for their Chop specialty. Keep a stockpile of Small Logs feeding into Lumber production at all times.

name: Brick
description: A sturdy fired brick made by applying the Burn specialty to Squishy Clay. Essential for stone buildings, ovens, and industrial structures.
howToObtain: Use the Burn specialty on Squishy Clay.
farmingTip: Ground-type Litter boxes produce Squishy Clay passively. Keep a Burn-specialty Pokemon busy converting clay to bricks for your construction projects.

name: Crystal Fragment
description: A shimmering piece of crystal broken from the large crystal formations in the mountains. Used in premium decorations and special crafting recipes.
howToObtain: Mine large crystal formations found in Rocky Ridges.
farmingTip: Large crystals in Rocky Ridges are the primary source. Sky Dream Island also has Crystal Fragments for supplemental farming.

name: Copper Ingot
description: A refined copper bar produced by smelting Copper Ore. Used in wiring, decorative fixtures, and mid-tier crafting recipes.
howToObtain: Smelt Copper Ore at a furnace or with a Smelting specialty Pokemon.
farmingTip: Set up a smelting station near your ore storage. Process copper in batches to keep your ingot supply ahead of your crafting needs.

name: Iron Ingot
description: A solid iron bar smelted from Iron Ore. The workhorse metal for tools, structural supports, and industrial crafting. Tinkaton can process these into Tinkagears.
howToObtain: Smelt Iron Ore at a furnace or with a Smelting specialty Pokemon.
farmingTip: Iron Ingots are in constant demand. Maintain a smelting pipeline from Rocky Ridges mining runs and Bleak Beach garbage recycling.

name: Gold Ingot
description: A gleaming gold bar smelted from Gold Ore. Used in premium furniture, luxury decorations, and some late-game crafting recipes.
howToObtain: Smelt Gold Ore at a furnace or with a Smelting specialty Pokemon.
farmingTip: Gold Ingots are needed less frequently than Iron but in larger quantities per recipe. Stockpile Gold Ore during mining runs.

name: Pokemetal
description: A refined bar of the mysterious Pokemetal unique to Pokopia. Required for the most advanced crafting recipes and endgame structures.
howToObtain: Smelt Pokemetal Fragments at a furnace or with a Smelting specialty Pokemon.
farmingTip: Pokemetal Fragments are scarce. Mine every Pokemetal block in Withered Wasteland, Sparkling Skylands, and Sky Dream Island to keep your supply up.

name: Paper
description: Clean, usable paper recycled from Wastepaper. Used in wallpaper recipes, decorations, and various crafting projects.
howToObtain: Use the Recycle ability on Wastepaper collected from Sparkling Skylands.
farmingTip: Sparkling Skylands has Wastepaper everywhere. Gather in bulk and recycle it all at once for efficient Paper production.

name: Glass
description: Clear glass produced by applying the Burn specialty to volcanic ash, sand, or sandstone. Used in windows, lanterns, and decorative crafting.
howToObtain: Use the Burn specialty on volcanic ash, sand, or sandstone blocks.
farmingTip: Sand and sandstone are plentiful in Bleak Beach and Rocky Ridges. Volcanic ash appears near lava areas. Keep a Burn-specialty Pokemon assigned to glass production.

name: Tinkagear
description: A precision-crafted gear component produced exclusively by Tinkaton processing Iron Ingots. Required for mechanical contraptions and advanced machinery recipes.
howToObtain: Have Tinkaton process Iron Ingots using its unique specialty.
farmingTip: Tinkaton is the only Pokemon that can make Tinkagears. Recruit one as soon as possible and keep it supplied with Iron Ingots.

name: Concrete
description: A heavy, durable building material produced by mixing Limestone in a Concrete Mixer. Essential for urban-style construction in Sparkling Skylands and Palette Town builds.
howToObtain: Process Limestone in a Concrete Mixer. Limestone is found in Sparkling Skylands.
farmingTip: Limestone is specific to Sparkling Skylands and Rock Peak Dream Island. Build a Concrete Mixer early in the Skylands phase to start stockpiling.

name: Aspear Berry Seeds
description: Seeds that grow into Aspear Berry plants. Aspear Berries are a key cooking ingredient used in several recipes.
howToObtain: Found while exploring, purchased from shops, or obtained from harvested Aspear Berry plants.
farmingTip: Plant these using Rototiller-prepared soil. Water daily for best growth. Save some berries to get more seeds rather than using them all for cooking.

name: Chesto Berry Seeds
description: Seeds for growing Chesto Berries, a dry berry used in cooking recipes and valued by certain Pokemon as gifts.
howToObtain: Found while exploring or purchased from shops.
farmingTip: Chesto Berries grow quickly and are useful in early cooking recipes. Plant a row alongside your other berry crops.

name: Leppa Berry Seeds
description: Seeds that produce Leppa Berries, a sweet red berry frequently used in cooking and as Pokemon gifts.
howToObtain: Found while exploring, especially in Wasteland Dream Island.
farmingTip: Wasteland Dream Island is particularly rich in Leppa Berry Seeds. Grab extras during Dream Island visits.

name: Pecha Berry Seeds
description: Seeds for growing sweet Pecha Berries. A staple cooking ingredient and popular gift for Pokemon.
howToObtain: Found while exploring or purchased from shops.
farmingTip: Pecha Berries are among the most used cooking ingredients. Dedicate a farm plot to continuous Pecha production.

name: Wheat Seeds
description: Seeds for growing wheat, a fundamental crop used in bread, pastries, and many cooking recipes.
howToObtain: Found while exploring or purchased from shops.
farmingTip: Wheat is used in a huge number of cooking recipes. Plant large fields and keep a constant supply growing. Use Rototiller to prepare soil efficiently.

name: Tomato Seeds
description: Seeds that grow into tomato plants. Tomatoes are a versatile cooking ingredient used in sauces, salads, and main dishes.
howToObtain: Found while exploring or purchased from shops.
farmingTip: Tomato plants produce multiple harvests before needing replanting. Keep them watered daily for maximum yield.

name: Bean Seeds
description: Seeds for growing beans, a nutritious crop used in hearty cooking recipes.
howToObtain: Found while exploring or purchased from shops.
farmingTip: Beans grow reliably and are used in several recipes that Chef Dente teaches at Rocky Ridges. Always keep some in stock.

name: Stardust
description: Shimmering cosmic dust found only on Dream Islands. A premium crafting material needed for the most special recipes and decorations in Pokopia.
howToObtain: Collected exclusively from Dream Islands. Cannot be found in the four main areas or Palette Town.
farmingTip: You can only visit one Dream Island per day via Drifloon. Plan your visits strategically and always gather all available Stardust during each trip.

name: Rainbow Feathers
description: Magnificent iridescent feathers shed by Ho-Oh. Among the rarest materials in all of Pokopia, used in legendary-tier crafting recipes.
howToObtain: Dropped by Ho-Oh. Requires building the Altar of Flame habitat in Palette Town to attract Ho-Oh.
farmingTip: Ho-Oh must be attracted by building and completing the Altar of Flame in Palette Town. Rainbow Feathers drop infrequently, so be patient.

name: Silver Feathers
description: Ethereal silver feathers from Lugia, shimmering with oceanic energy. Required for some of the most powerful crafting recipes in the game.
howToObtain: Dropped by Lugia. Requires building a special ocean habitat to attract Lugia.
location: Palette Town or Bleak Beach area
farmingTip: Lugia is one of the hardest legendaries to attract. Build the required habitat and check regularly for Silver Feather drops.

name: Mysterious Slate
description: An ancient stone tablet inscribed with mysterious symbols. There are 27 total hidden throughout Pokopia, and collecting all of them is the key to encountering Mew.
howToObtain: Found hidden throughout all areas of Pokopia. 27 total scattered across the game world.
location: All areas (27 total throughout the game)
farmingTip: These are fixed collectibles, not farmable. Explore every corner of every area thoroughly. Some are hidden behind puzzles or in hard-to-reach spots. Collecting all 27 unlocks Mew.

name: Strange Strings
description: Unusual glowing filaments recovered from rare glowing blocks. Their origin is unknown, but they are prized by collectors and crafters alike.
howToObtain: Rare drop from glowing blocks found in caves and underground areas.
farmingTip: Glowing blocks are uncommon and the drop rate for Strange Strings is low. Mine every glowing block you encounter across all areas.

name: Armor Fragment
description: A piece of ancient armor material found in rare glowing blocks. Used in special crafting recipes for durable structures and decorations.
howToObtain: Rare drop from glowing blocks found in caves and underground areas.
farmingTip: Like Strange Strings, these drop from glowing blocks at a low rate. Prioritize mining any glowing blocks you spot for a chance at this rare material.

name: Simple Salad
description: A basic leafy salad made at the Chopping Board. The first recipe you learn from Chef Dente. Upgrades your Leafage ability when consumed by a Pokemon.
howToObtain: Cook at the Chopping Board using Leaves and basic vegetables.
farmingTip: Simple Salad is the easiest food to mass-produce. Keep Leaves stocked and chop salads in bulk to always have Leafage upgrades ready.

name: Leppa Salad
description: A sweet salad featuring Leppa Berries over fresh greens. Prepared at the Chopping Board and upgrades Leafage with a sweet flavor boost.
howToObtain: Cook at the Chopping Board using Leppa Berries and Leaves.
farmingTip: Farm Leppa Berries from your garden to keep a steady supply. Sweet-flavored foods are preferred by many Pokemon.

name: Seaweed Salad
description: A fresh ocean-flavored salad using harvested Seaweed. Made at the Chopping Board and provides a salty Leafage upgrade.
howToObtain: Cook at the Chopping Board using Seaweed and Leaves.
farmingTip: Requires Seaweed from Bleak Beach water areas. Pair Seaweed gathering with your beach cleanup runs for efficiency.

name: Crouton Salad
description: A hearty salad topped with toasted bread croutons. The most filling salad recipe, providing a strong Leafage upgrade.
howToObtain: Cook at the Chopping Board using Wheat, Leaves, and a Berry.
farmingTip: Requires Wheat from your farm. This is the best salad for maximizing Leafage power, so prioritize it once your wheat supply is stable.

name: Simple Soup
description: A warm, basic broth cooked in the Cooking Pot. The entry-level soup recipe that upgrades your Water Gun ability.
howToObtain: Cook at the Cooking Pot using water ingredients and basic vegetables.
farmingTip: Simple Soup requires minimal ingredients. Cook it in bulk early on to keep your Water Gun powered up for cleaning pollution.

name: Seaweed Soup
description: A savory ocean broth made with fresh Seaweed in the Cooking Pot. Provides a salty Water Gun upgrade.
howToObtain: Cook at the Cooking Pot using Seaweed and water ingredients.
farmingTip: Combine Seaweed gathering at Bleak Beach with soup cooking sessions. Water Gun upgrades help immensely with beach cleanup progress.

name: Mushroom Soup
description: A rich, earthy soup made with Glowing Mushrooms in the Cooking Pot. Provides a bitter Water Gun upgrade favored by certain Pokemon.
howToObtain: Cook at the Cooking Pot using Glowing Mushrooms and basic ingredients.
farmingTip: Glowing Mushrooms from caves double as soup ingredients. Grab extras during mining runs specifically for cooking.

name: Electrifying Soup
description: A tingling, energizing soup with a spicy kick. Made in the Cooking Pot using Electric-type Pokemon litter ingredients. Provides a powerful Water Gun upgrade.
howToObtain: Cook at the Cooking Pot using special ingredients from Electric-type Pokemon Litter boxes.
farmingTip: Electric-type habitats like Pikachu and Mareep produce the special ingredients needed. This is one of the strongest Water Gun upgrades available.

name: Simple Bread
description: A basic loaf baked in the Bread Oven. The first bread recipe available that upgrades your Cut ability when consumed.
howToObtain: Bake at the Bread Oven using Wheat.
farmingTip: Wheat is the only ingredient needed. Keep your wheat farm productive and bake bread in large batches for consistent Cut upgrades.

name: Leppa Bread
description: Sweet bread baked with Leppa Berries folded into the dough. A sweet-flavored Cut upgrade baked in the Bread Oven.
howToObtain: Bake at the Bread Oven using Wheat and Leppa Berries.
farmingTip: Leppa Berries add the sweet flavor many Pokemon prefer. Bake Leppa Bread as gifts alongside using it for Cut upgrades.

name: Carrot Bread
description: Nutritious bread made with fresh carrots baked into the loaf. Provides a dry-flavored Cut upgrade from the Bread Oven.
howToObtain: Bake at the Bread Oven using Wheat and Carrots.
farmingTip: Carrots are available once you unlock the Rocky Ridges farm plots. The dry flavor makes this bread ideal for Pokemon that prefer dry foods.

name: Fluffy Bread
description: An extraordinarily soft and light bread made with Fluff and premium Wheat. The highest-tier bread recipe, providing the strongest Cut upgrade.
howToObtain: Bake at the Bread Oven using Wheat, Fluff, and Honey.
farmingTip: Requires Fluff from Mareep habitats and Honey from Combee. This is the strongest Cut upgrade bread, worth the extra ingredient investment.

name: Hamburger Steak
description: A hearty ground patty cooked in the Frying Pan. The basic hamburger steak recipe that upgrades your Rock Smash ability.
howToObtain: Cook at the Frying Pan using Beans and basic ingredients.
farmingTip: Beans are the primary ingredient. Keep a dedicated bean farm running to ensure you always have Hamburger Steaks for Rock Smash upgrades.

name: Mushroom Hamburger Steak
description: A savory hamburger steak topped with sauteed Glowing Mushrooms. Provides a bitter-flavored Rock Smash upgrade from the Frying Pan.
howToObtain: Cook at the Frying Pan using Beans and Glowing Mushrooms.
farmingTip: Combine cave mushroom foraging with mining trips. The bitter flavor of this steak is preferred by Rock and Ground-type Pokemon.

name: Tomato Hamburger Steak
description: A juicy hamburger steak smothered in fresh tomato sauce. Made in the Frying Pan with a sour flavor that upgrades Rock Smash.
howToObtain: Cook at the Frying Pan using Beans and Tomatoes.
farmingTip: Tomatoes from your farm pair perfectly with Beans. The sour flavor appeals to many Water and Grass-type Pokemon.

name: Vibrant Hamburger Steak
description: The ultimate hamburger steak bursting with color and flavor from multiple berry types. The strongest Rock Smash upgrade food available.
howToObtain: Cook at the Frying Pan using Beans, multiple Berry types, and Honey.
farmingTip: Requires a variety of berries plus Honey. Worth the complex ingredients for the strongest Rock Smash upgrade. Save these for tough mining challenges.

name: Workbench
description: The essential crafting station for building furniture, decorations, and other items. Every settlement needs at least one Workbench to function.
howToObtain: Craft using Lumber and Stone. One of the first recipes taught in the tutorial.
farmingTip: Build your first Workbench immediately after gathering initial materials. Place it centrally in your base for easy access during building sessions.

name: Storage Box
description: A wooden chest for storing materials and items. Expands your storage capacity significantly and can be placed anywhere in your settlement.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Lumber and Iron Ingots.
farmingTip: Build multiple Storage Boxes early. Organize by material type: one for ores, one for processed goods, one for food ingredients. Saves time later.

name: Cooking Station
description: A full cooking setup that includes a Chopping Board, Cooking Pot, Bread Oven, and Frying Pan. Unlocks all food recipes when placed.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Brick, Iron Ingots, and Lumber.
farmingTip: Build this as soon as you have Bricks and Iron Ingots. Food upgrades to Leafage, Water Gun, Cut, and Rock Smash are critical for progression.

name: Smelting Furnace
description: A high-temperature furnace for smelting ores into ingots and processing raw materials. Essential for metalworking in mid-game.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Brick, Stone, and Copper Ore.
farmingTip: Place near your ore storage for efficient processing. A Smelting-specialty Pokemon assigned to this furnace will process ores automatically.

name: Loom
description: A textile crafting station for weaving Fluff, Twine, and other fibers into fabrics, rugs, and soft furnishings.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Lumber, Twine, and Vine Rope.
farmingTip: The Loom unlocks fabric-based recipes including rugs, curtains, and cushion covers. Pair it with Mareep Fluff production for maximum output.

name: Jeweler's Table
description: A precision crafting station for working with Crystal Fragments, Gold Ingots, and other precious materials into jewelry and luxury decorations.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Gold Ingots, Crystal Fragments, and Lumber.
farmingTip: Unlocks high-value decoration recipes. The Crystal Chandelier and other premium items require this station. Worth the Gold Ingot investment.

name: Pikachu Sofa
description: An adorable sofa shaped like a lounging Pikachu, complete with red cheek cushions and a lightning-bolt tail backrest. A beloved Pokemon-themed furniture piece.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Lumber, Fluff, and Yellow Paint.
farmingTip: Requires Yellow Paint from crushing Aspear Berries. Plan your berry farming to include Aspear Berries specifically for this recipe.

name: Snorlax Bean Bag
description: A massive, supremely comfortable bean bag chair modeled after a sleeping Snorlax. Pokemon and players alike can rest on its enormous belly.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using a large quantity of Fluff, Twine, and Blue Paint.
farmingTip: This recipe requires a lot of Fluff. Build extra Mareep habitats before attempting this craft. The result is worth the material investment.

name: Avalugg Table
description: A stunning ice-crystal table inspired by Avalugg's flat, icy body. Its translucent surface shimmers in the light. A statement piece for any room.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Crystal Fragments, Glass, and Iron Ingots.
farmingTip: Crystal Fragments from Rocky Ridges are the key ingredient. Glass adds the translucent effect. Pairs beautifully with the Crystal Chandelier.

name: Slowpoke Rug
description: A soft, pink rug shaped like a sprawled-out Slowpoke. Woven at the Loom, it adds a cozy and whimsical touch to any room.
howToObtain: Weave at the Loom using Fluff, Twine, and Pink Paint.
farmingTip: Requires the Loom and Pink Paint from crushed Pecha Berries. One of the most popular decorations for Pokemon-themed rooms.

name: Electrode Lamp
description: A spherical lamp designed to look like Electrode, glowing with electric energy. Provides bright lighting and adds personality to any room.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Glass, Copper Ingots, and Red Paint.
farmingTip: Glass and Copper Ingots are the main ingredients. The Red Paint gives it the classic Electrode look. Place near entrances for both style and function.

name: Wooden Chair
description: A simple but sturdy wooden chair. One of the first furniture items you can craft, essential for furnishing homes and Pokemon Centers.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Lumber.
farmingTip: Quick and cheap to craft. Build several early on to furnish the Pokemon Center and starter homes. Can be painted later for customization.

name: Wooden Table
description: A basic wooden table for dining or display. Pairs naturally with Wooden Chairs for a complete furniture set.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Lumber and Sturdy Sticks.
farmingTip: Craft alongside Wooden Chairs for matching sets. Tables can also serve as display surfaces for decorative items.

name: Wooden Bed
description: A cozy wooden bed frame with a Fluff mattress. Required in every home to make it a valid living space for Pokemon residents.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Lumber, Fluff, and Twine.
farmingTip: Every home needs a bed for a Pokemon to move in. Prioritize bed crafting when building new houses to attract residents quickly.

name: Bookshelf
description: A tall wooden bookshelf filled with volumes about Pokemon habitats and Pokopia lore. Adds an intellectual charm to any room.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Lumber, Paper, and Iron Ingots.
farmingTip: Paper is the bottleneck ingredient, requiring Wastepaper from Sparkling Skylands. Stockpile Paper during Skylands exploration runs.

name: Dresser
description: A handsome wooden dresser with multiple drawers. Functions as both a decorative piece and extra storage in furnished rooms.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Lumber, Iron Ingots, and Copper Ingots.
farmingTip: The metal hardware requires both Iron and Copper Ingots. A good intermediate furniture piece that adds storage to homes.

name: Community Box
description: A shared storage container accessible by all Pokemon in your settlement. Place in public areas so residents can deposit and retrieve items freely.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Lumber, Iron Ingots, and Brick.
farmingTip: Place one near your Workbench and another near farming areas. Pokemon will use these to store materials they find while exploring.

name: Pokemon Center Kit
description: A comprehensive construction kit for rebuilding the Pokemon Center in each area. Each of the four main areas has a ruined Pokemon Center that can be restored with this kit.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Lumber, Brick, Iron Ingots, Glass, and Copper Ingots.
farmingTip: Rebuilding each area's Pokemon Center is a major milestone. Gather all materials before starting, as construction cannot be paused. Restored Centers provide 3D Printing access.

name: Basic House Kit
description: A construction kit for building a simple wooden home. The starter housing option that attracts Pokemon residents to your settlement.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Lumber, Stone, and Vine Rope.
farmingTip: Build Basic Houses first to get Pokemon moved in quickly. You can upgrade them later to fancier designs. Each house needs furniture inside to count as livable.

name: Stone Cottage Kit
description: A construction kit for building a charming stone cottage with a brick chimney. A mid-tier housing upgrade that Pokemon residents prefer over basic homes.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Stone, Brick, Lumber, and Glass.
farmingTip: Stone Cottages increase Pokemon happiness compared to Basic Houses. Prioritize upgrading homes in areas where you want to attract rarer Pokemon.

name: Luxury Villa Kit
description: A premium construction kit for building an elaborate multi-room villa. The highest-tier residential building, required to attract the most discerning Pokemon residents.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Lumber, Brick, Gold Ingots, Glass, and Pokemetal.
farmingTip: Luxury Villas require Gold Ingots and Pokemetal, making them endgame builds. Reserve these for attracting legendary or extremely rare Pokemon.

name: Grass Habitat Kit
description: A habitat construction kit designed to attract Grass-type Pokemon. Features lush garden beds, vine trellises, and mossy stones.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Lumber, Leaves, Vine Rope, and Squishy Clay.
farmingTip: Build in areas with natural vegetation for maximum effectiveness. Grass habitats attract Bulbasaur, Bellsprout, and other Grass-types that provide useful Litter drops.

name: Freezing Chambers Kit
description: A legendary habitat construction kit for building the Freezing Chambers in Palette Town. This unique structure attracts the legendary bird Articuno when completed.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Pokemetal, Crystal Fragments, Glass, Iron Ingots, and Stardust.
farmingTip: This is one of three legendary habitat kits. Pokemetal and Stardust are the hardest ingredients to gather. Articuno will visit once the Freezing Chambers are fully built and furnished.

name: Abandoned Power Plant Kit
description: A legendary habitat construction kit for rebuilding the Abandoned Power Plant in Palette Town. This electrifying structure attracts the legendary bird Zapdos.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Pokemetal, Tinkagears, Copper Ingots, Iron Ingots, and Stardust.
farmingTip: Tinkagears from Tinkaton are essential. Start stockpiling Tinkagears and Pokemetal well before attempting this build. Zapdos arrives once the Power Plant is complete.

name: Altar of Flame Kit
description: A legendary habitat construction kit for constructing the Altar of Flame in Palette Town. This sacred structure attracts the legendary bird Moltres and eventually Ho-Oh.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Pokemetal, Gold Ingots, Brick, Stardust, and Rare Pokemetal Fragments.
farmingTip: The most expensive legendary habitat, requiring Rare Pokemetal Fragments. Moltres arrives first; Ho-Oh requires all three legendary birds to be present in Palette Town.

name: Crystal Chandelier
description: A dazzling chandelier crafted from Crystal Fragments and Gold Ingots. Illuminates rooms with prismatic light and serves as the ultimate luxury ceiling decoration.
howToObtain: Craft at the Jeweler's Table using Crystal Fragments, Gold Ingots, and Twine.
farmingTip: Requires the Jeweler's Table. Crystal Fragments are the main bottleneck. This is the most impressive lighting decoration and dramatically improves room ratings.

name: Town Map
description: A framed map of Pokopia showing all four main areas and Palette Town. A wall decoration that helps Pokemon navigate and adds a scholarly touch to rooms.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Paper, Lumber, and Twine.
farmingTip: Paper from recycled Wastepaper is needed. A nice decorative piece for Pokemon Center lobbies and residential homes alike.

name: Mossy Wall Tapestry
description: A living wall decoration woven with moss, vines, and Glowing Mushrooms. Creates a natural, enchanted atmosphere in any room.
howToObtain: Weave at the Loom using Vine Rope, Glowing Mushrooms, Leaves, and Twine.
farmingTip: The Glowing Mushrooms give this tapestry a soft bioluminescent glow at night. Pairs well with nature-themed rooms and Grass-type Pokemon habitats.

name: Arcade Machine
description: A retro arcade cabinet featuring classic Pokemon minigames. A functional decoration that Pokemon residents can interact with for entertainment and happiness boosts.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Iron Ingots, Tinkagears, Glass, and Copper Ingots.
farmingTip: Tinkagears and Glass are the key components. Place in Pokemon Centers or community areas for maximum resident happiness. Pokemon love playing the minigames.

name: Red Paint
description: Vibrant red paint produced by using the Crush specialty on Cheri Berries. Used to color furniture, walls, and decorations in bold red hues.
howToObtain: Use the Crush specialty on Cheri Berries.
farmingTip: Cheri Berries are common and easy to farm. Grow a dedicated patch for consistent Red Paint production. Red is used in many Pokemon-themed furniture recipes.

name: Blue Paint
description: Deep blue paint created by crushing Oran Berries with the Crush specialty. Essential for water-themed decorations and several Pokemon furniture recipes.
howToObtain: Use the Crush specialty on Oran Berries.
farmingTip: Oran Berries are plentiful in most areas. Blue Paint is needed for the Snorlax Bean Bag, water-themed rooms, and many wall colors.

name: Green Paint
description: Natural green paint made by crushing Rawst Berries. Perfect for nature-themed builds, Grass-type habitat accents, and forest-style decorations.
howToObtain: Use the Crush specialty on Rawst Berries.
farmingTip: Rawst Berries grow quickly and are widely available. Green Paint complements the natural aesthetic of Withered Wasteland builds.

name: Yellow Paint
description: Bright yellow paint produced by crushing Aspear Berries. A key ingredient for Electric-type themed builds and the popular Pikachu Sofa.
howToObtain: Use the Crush specialty on Aspear Berries.
farmingTip: Aspear Berries from your farm are the source. Yellow is required for the Pikachu Sofa and Electrode Lamp color variants. Keep a steady supply growing.

name: Pink Paint
description: Soft pink paint made by crushing Pecha Berries. Used in Fairy-type themed decorations, the Slowpoke Rug, and pastel-colored furniture.
howToObtain: Use the Crush specialty on Pecha Berries.
farmingTip: Pecha Berries are one of the most common berries. Pink Paint is needed for many cute Pokemon-themed items. Double up your Pecha farm for cooking and painting.

name: Cyan Paint
description: A cool cyan paint made by mixing Blue and Green paint processes. Used in Ice-type themed builds, sky decorations, and ocean-style furnishings.
howToObtain: Use the Crush specialty on a combination of Oran and Rawst Berries.
farmingTip: Requires two berry types to produce. Cyan is perfect for Bleak Beach themed rooms and Ice-type habitat accents. Less common than primary colors.

name: White Paint
description: Pure white paint, a rare product of crushing Leppa Berries. Highly sought after for clean modern builds, highlights, and mixing with other paints for lighter shades.
howToObtain: Use the Crush specialty on Leppa Berries. Rare drop rate.
farmingTip: White Paint has a low yield from Leppa Berries, making it one of the rarer paints. Dedicate a large Leppa Berry farm and crush in bulk for best results.

name: Black Paint
description: Deep black paint, rarely produced by crushing Aspear Berries. Essential for dark-themed builds, Gothic decorations, and creating striking color contrasts.
howToObtain: Use the Crush specialty on Aspear Berries. Rare drop rate.
farmingTip: Black Paint is the rarest paint alongside White. Aspear Berries usually yield Yellow Paint, with Black being an uncommon alternative. Crush large batches and hope for luck.

name: 3D Printer
description: An advanced fabrication device found at rebuilt Pokemon Centers. Take photos of items in the world, then use the 3D Printer to craft replica furniture and decorations from those photos.
howToObtain: Available at rebuilt Pokemon Centers. Requires the Pokemon Center to be fully restored.
farmingTip: Photograph every interesting item and decoration you find while exploring. The 3D Printer can reproduce them using basic materials, unlocking unique items you cannot craft any other way.

name: Concrete Mixer
description: An industrial mixing station for producing Concrete from Limestone. Unlocked upon reaching Sparkling Skylands, enabling urban-style construction.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Iron Ingots, Tinkagears, and Brick. Unlocked when Sparkling Skylands is accessible.
farmingTip: Build immediately upon reaching Sparkling Skylands. Concrete is needed for most Skylands-era construction and several late-game buildings in Palette Town.

name: Sprinkler System
description: An automated watering system for your farm plots. Once installed, it waters crops daily without needing a Water Gun Pokemon, freeing up your companions for other tasks.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Copper Ingots, Tinkagears, and Iron Ingots.
farmingTip: A game-changing quality-of-life upgrade. Install one per farm zone to automate watering entirely. This frees your Water-type companion for exploration and pollution cleanup.

name: Flower Planter
description: A decorative planter box for growing flowers from Flower Seeds. Place along paths, around buildings, and near habitats to beautify your settlement.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Lumber, Squishy Clay, and Flower Seeds.
farmingTip: Flower Planters increase area beauty scores and attract Bug-type Pokemon. Line your main paths with these for both aesthetics and practical benefits.

name: Seashell Wind Chime
description: A delicate wind chime crafted from polished Seashells and Twine. Makes gentle tinkling sounds in the breeze and adds a coastal ambiance to outdoor spaces.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Seashells, Twine, and Sturdy Sticks.
farmingTip: Seashells from Bleak Beach are easy to stockpile once the area is cleaned up. Wind Chimes are a popular outdoor decoration that complements beach-themed builds.

name: Sea Glass Lantern
description: A glowing lantern made from polished Sea Glass Fragments and Copper Ingots. Casts soft, multicolored light through its frosted glass panels.
howToObtain: Craft at the Workbench using Sea Glass Fragments, Copper Ingots, and Twine.
farmingTip: Sea Glass Fragments from cleaned Bleak Beach tiles are the key ingredient. These lanterns look stunning along coastal paths and in ocean-themed rooms.

name: Healthy Soup
description: A nutritious green soup packed with fresh vegetables and Leaves. Prepared in the Cooking Pot, it provides a balanced Water Gun upgrade with no dominant flavor.
howToObtain: Cook at the Cooking Pot using Leaves, Beans, and Tomatoes.
farmingTip: Uses common farm ingredients making it easy to produce in quantity. The balanced flavor means any Pokemon will happily eat it for the Water Gun boost.

name: Bread Bowl
description: A hollowed-out bread loaf filled with rich soup. Combines the Cut upgrade of bread with the Water Gun upgrade of soup for a dual-ability boost. The ultimate cooking achievement.
howToObtain: Bake at the Bread Oven using Wheat, Beans, Tomatoes, and Honey.
farmingTip: The only food that upgrades two abilities at once (Cut and Water Gun). Requires more ingredients but the dual boost makes it incredibly valuable for tough areas.
